similar_questions.title: Find the XOR of Numbers Which Appear Twice

discussion_posts.name: aceanojjj
discussion_posts.content: I was like \'aha a piece of cake\', until I read the last requirement \uD83E\uDD79

discussion_posts.name: daringcalf
discussion_posts.content: I have a bit that needs to be manipulated.

discussion_posts.name: saadtaame
discussion_posts.content: Has anybody solved this problem by using constant space? My solution uses a map to keep track of the counts of each element in the array.

discussion_posts.name: robotqueen
discussion_posts.content: If this is not a hard question, I dont know what is. I don`t think anyone less than an expert on bit manipulations can fully code this solution in a white board interview (talking about solving it at linear time and constant space of course).

discussion_posts.name: shivamchaurasia2912
discussion_posts.content:
My First Approach was counting set bits :-
Eg :- [2,2,2,3,4,4,4]
binary form :- 010
                       010
                       010
                       011
                       100
                       100 
                       100
Total :-            3 4 1
So, 3 is divisible by 3 hence "0" but four is not divisible "1" and same with "1" . The final answer will be "011" = 3.

discussion_posts.name: MahekSavani
discussion_posts.content:
Easier to solve from maths perspective:
Find all the distinct numbers (set), multiply by 3 (because all the other number repeats 3 time), so the sum of that set will have total of original nums + 2*(number which is repeating only once)

discussion_posts.name: cecilia5
discussion_posts.content: The reason xor works for the case of every element appear twice and only element appear once is that the xor function is "bit"-wise annihilating for when applied to the same element twice, commutative and associative.\n\nWhat kind of operator can do "tri"-wise, annihilating for when apply to the same element 3 times, commutative and associative? What is "tri"-wise anyway?

discussion_posts.name: Yan-Zhelanov
discussion_posts.content:
1. Keep two variables, `ones` and `twos`, to hold the bits that have appeared one time and two times, respectively. Iterate through the array. For each number:
- Update twos with bits that have appeared two times now. These are the bits which were in ones earlier and in the current number.
- Update ones with bits that have appeared one time. However, you need to remove those bits which are in twos.
2. If a bit is present in twos, it means it has already appeared two times, so remove these bits from ones and twos.
3. After the end of array traversal, ones will hold the bits of the number that appears exactly once in the array. Other numbers' bits will get cancelled out as we remove them when they appear the third time.

discussion_posts.name: yinjiecheng
discussion_posts.content:
From the [best answer][1] i'v known how to solve the problem "every element appears N times except for one", but what if the opposite?

Every element appears only one time except for one, how to find the only one element that appears 2(and 3 to N) times? Also requires a linear runtime complexity, and a linear runtime complexity.


  [1]: http://oj.leetcode.com/discuss/857/constant-space-solution
